(typeof document === \"undefined\") return;\n    const root = document.documentElement;\n    root.setAttribute(\"data-theme\", theme.themeMode);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-primary\", theme.primaryColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-accent\", theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-bg\", theme.bgColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-surface\", theme.surfaceColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-elevated\", theme.elevatedColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-primary\", theme.textPrimary);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-secondary\", theme.textSecondary);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-muted\", theme.textMuted);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-on-primary\", \"#ffffff\");\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-border\", theme.borderColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-success\", theme.successColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-warning\", theme.warningColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-error\", theme.errorColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-glass-bg\", theme.glassBg);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-primary-glow\", theme.primaryGlow);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-primary\", theme.chartPrimary || theme.primaryColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-secondary\", theme.chartSecondary || theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-accent\", theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-radius\", `${theme.radius}px`);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-card-radius\", `${theme.radius}px`);\n    let fontVar = theme.font;\n    if (theme.font === \"Geist\") fontVar = \"var(--font-geist)\";\n    if (theme.font === \"JetBrains Mono\") fontVar = \"var(--font-geist-mono)\";\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-font\", For backwards compatibility, we need to check we're in a\n        // browser context before continuing.\n        if (typeof self !== 'undefined' &&\n            // AMP / No-JS mode does not inject these helpers:\n            '$RefreshHelpers$' in self) {\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var currentExports = module.exports;\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var prevSignature = (_b = (_a = module.hot.data) === null || _a === void 0 ? void 0 : _a.prevSignature) !== null && _b !== void 0 ? _b : null;\n            // This cannot happen in MainTemplate because the exports mismatch between\n            // templating and execution.\n            self.$RefreshHelpers$.registerExportsForReactRefresh(currentExports, module.id);\n            // A module can be accepted automatically based on its exports, e.g. when\n            // it is a Refresh Boundary.\n            if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.isReactRefreshBoundary(currentExports)) {\n                // Save the previous exports signature on update so we can compare the boundary\n                // signatures. We avoid saving exports themselves since it causes memory leaks (https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/53797)\n                module.hot.dispose(function (data) {\n                    data.prevSignature =\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports);\n                });\n                // Unconditionally accept an update to this module, we'll check if it's\n                // still a Refresh Boundary later.\n                // @ts-ignore importMeta is replaced in the loader\n                module.hot.accept();\n                // This field is set when the previous version of this module was a\n                // Refresh Boundary, letting us know we need to check for invalidation or\n                // enqueue an update.\n                if (prevSignature !== null) {\n                    // A boundary can become ineligible if its exports are incompatible\n                    // with the previous exports.\n                    //\n                    // For example, if you add/remove/change exports, we'll want to\n                    // re-execute the importing modules, and force those components to\n                    // re-render. Similarly, if you convert a class component to a\n                    // function, we want to invalidate the boundary.\n                    if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.shouldInvalidateReactRefreshBoundary(prevSignature, self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports))) {\n                        module.hot.invalidate();\n                    }\n                    else {\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.scheduleUpdate();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            else {\n                // Since we just executed the code for the module, it's possible that the\n                // new exports made it ineligible for being a boundary.\n                // We only care about the case when we were _previously_ a boundary,\n                // because we already accepted this update (accidental side effect).\n                var isNoLongerABoundary = prevSignature !== null;\n                if (isNoLongerABoundary) {\n                    module.hot.invalidate();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    })();\n//# sourceURL=[module]\n//# sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;charset=utf-8;base64,\n//# sourceURL=webpack-internal:///(app-pages-browser)/./providers/ThemeProvider.tsx\n"));

\"p9GawxKqUFLfKJz0xlMCJwL3oRo=\");\n_c = Hero;\nvar _c;\n$RefreshReg$(_c, \"Hero\");\n\n\n;\n    // Wrapped in an IIFE to avoid polluting the global scope\n    ;\n    (function () {\n        var _a, _b;\n        // Legacy CSS implementations will `eval` browser code in a Node.js context\n        // to extract CSS. For backwards compatibility, we need to check we're in a\n        // browser context before continuing.\n        if (typeof self !== 'undefined' &&\n            // AMP / No-JS mode does not inject these helpers:\n            '$RefreshHelpers$' in self) {\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var currentExports = module.exports;\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var prevSignature = (_b = (_a = module.hot.data) === null || _a === void 0 ? void 0 : _a.prevSignature) !== null && _b !== void 0 ? _b : null;\n            // This cannot happen in MainTemplate because the exports mismatch between\n            // templating and execution.\n            self.$RefreshHelpers$.registerExportsForReactRefresh(currentExports, module.id);\n            // A module can be accepted automatically based on its exports, e.g. when\n            // it is a Refresh Boundary.\n            if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.isReactRefreshBoundary(currentExports)) {\n                // Save the previous exports signature on update so we can compare the boundary\n                // signatures. We avoid saving exports themselves since it causes memory leaks (https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/53797)\n                module.hot.dispose(function (data) {\n                    data.prevSignature =\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports);\n                });\n                // Unconditionally accept an update to this module, we'll check if it's\n                // still a Refresh Boundary later.\n                // @ts-ignore importMeta is replaced in the loader\n                module.hot.accept();\n                // This field is set when the previous version of this module was a\n                // Refresh Boundary, letting us know we need to check for invalidation or\n                // enqueue an update.\n                if (prevSignature !== null) {\n                    // A boundary can become ineligible if its exports are incompatible\n                    // with the previous exports.\n                    //\n                    // For example, if you add/remove/change exports, we'll want to\n                    // re-execute the importing modules, and force those components to\n                    // re-render. Similarly, if you convert a class component to a\n                    // function, we want to invalidate the boundary.\n                    if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.shouldInvalidateReactRefreshBoundary(prevSignature, self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports))) {\n                        module.hot.invalidate();\n                    }\n                    else {\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.scheduleUpdate();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            else {\n                // Since we just executed the code for the module, it's possible that the\n                // new exports made it ineligible for being a boundary.\n                // We only care about the case when we were _previously_ a boundary,\n                // because we already accepted this update (accidental side effect).\n                var isNoLongerABoundary = prevSignature !== null;\n                if (isNoLongerABoundary) {\n                    module.hot.invalidate();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    })();\n//# sourceURL=[module]\n//# sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;charset=utf-8;base64,\n//# sourceURL=webpack-internal:///(app-pages-browser)/./components/sections/hero.tsx\n"));

ThemeContext = /*#__PURE__*/ (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.createContext)(undefined);\n// Helper function to apply CSS variables directly to the DOM\nconst applyThemeToDOM = (theme)=>{\n    if (typeof document === \"undefined\") return;\n    const root = document.documentElement;\n    root.setAttribute(\"data-theme\", theme.themeMode);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-primary\", theme.primaryColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-accent\", theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-bg\", theme.bgColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-surface\", theme.surfaceColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-elevated\", theme.elevatedColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-primary\", theme.textPrimary);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-secondary\", theme.textSecondary);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-muted\", theme.textMuted);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-on-primary\", \"#ffffff\");\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-border\", theme.borderColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-success\", theme.successColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-warning\", theme.warningColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-error\", theme.errorColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-glass-bg\", theme.glassBg);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-primary-glow\", theme.primaryGlow);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-primary\", theme.chartPrimary || theme.primaryColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-secondary\", theme.chartSecondary || theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-accent\", theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-radius\", \"\".concat(theme.radius, \"px\"));\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-card-radius\", \"\".concat(theme.radius, \"px\"));\n    let fontVar = theme.font;\n    if (theme.font === \"Geist\") fontVar = \"var(--font-geist)\";\n    if (theme.font === \"JetBrains Mono\") fontVar = \"var(--font-geist-mono)\";\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-font\", fontVar);\n};\nconst useIsomorphicLayoutEffect =  true ? (react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1___default().useLayoutEffect) : 0;\nconst getInitialTheme = ()=>{\n    if (true) {\n        try {\n            const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem(\"prism-theme\");\n            if (savedTheme) {\n                return JSON.parse(savedTheme);\n            }\n        } catch (e) {\n            console.error(\"Failed to parse saved theme from localStorage\", e);\n        }\n    }\n    const firstPresetId = AVAILABLE_PRESETS[0].id;\n    return {\n        ...baseTheme,\n        ...presets[firstPresetId],\n        activePreset: firstPresetId\n    };\n};\nfunction ThemeProvider(param) {\n    let { children } = param;\n    _s();\n    const [themeState, setThemeState] = (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useState)(getInitialTheme);\n    const [mounted, setMounted] = (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useState)(false);\n    // useIsomorphicLayoutEffect runs synchronously before browser paint\n    useIsomorphicLayoutEffect(()=>{\n        let currentTheme = getInitialTheme();\n        setThemeState(currentTheme);\n        // Immediately apply CSS variables to the DOM before rendering frame\n        applyThemeToDOM(currentTheme);\n        setMounted(true);\n    }, []);\n    // Save to localStorage and apply CSS vars whenever themeState changes\n    (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useEffect)(()=>{\n        if (!mounted) return;\n        localStorage.setItem(\"prism-theme\", JSON.stringify(themeState));\n        applyThemeToDOM(themeState);\n    }, [\n        themeState,\n        mounted\n    ]);\n    const setTheme = (updates)=>{\n        setThemeState((prev)=>({\n                ...prev,\n                ...updates\n            }));\n    };\n    const applyPreset = (presetId)=>{\n        const presetTheme = presets[presetId];\n        if (presetTheme) {\n            setThemeState((prev)=>({\n                    ...prev,\n                    ...presetTheme,\n                    activePreset: presetId\n                }));\n        }\n    };\n    // We only render children once mounted to avoid hydration mismatch with text/content that depends on the theme.\n    // Wait, if we return null, there is a FOUC (white screen).\n    // The requirement says \"Avoid flashes of incorrect theme during page load\". \n    // Next.js hydration will just give warnings in dev, so we can suppress them or ignore them.\n    // Let's render children immediately. The CSS variables are applied synchronously above so there is no visual FOUC!\n    return /*#__PURE__*/ (0,react_jsx_dev_runtime__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_0__.jsxDEV)(ThemeContext.Provider, {\n        value: {\n            ...themeState,\n            setTheme,\n            applyPreset\n        },\n        children: /*#__PURE__*/ (0,react_jsx_dev_runtime__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_0__.jsxDEV)(\"div\", {\n            style: {\n                display: \"contents\"\n            },\n            suppressHydrationWarning: true,\n            children: children\n        }, void 0, false, {\n            fileName: \"C:\\\\Users\\\\Shaik Afreen\\\\OneDrive\\\\Desktop\\\\prism-v2-main\\\\providers\\\\ThemeProvider.tsx\",\n            lineNumber: 227,\n            columnNumber: 7\n        }, this)\n    }, void 0, false, {\n        fileName: \"C:\\\\Users\\\\Shaik Afreen\\\\OneDrive\\\\Desktop\\\\prism-v2-main\\\\providers\\\\ThemeProvider.tsx\",\n        lineNumber: 226,\n        columnNumber: 5\n    }, this);\n}\n_s(ThemeProvider, \"ImMjQcN2HTuMEUSRFxJ3tGNTIr0=\", false, function() {\n    return [\n        useIsomorphicLayoutEffect\n    ];\n});\n_c = ThemeProvider;\nfunction useTheme() {\n    _s1();\n    const context = (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useContext)(ThemeContext);\n    if (context === undefined) {\n        throw new Error(\"useTheme must be used within a ThemeProvider\");\n    }\n    return context;\n}\n_s1(useTheme, \"b9L3QQ+jgeyIrH0NfHrJ8nn7VMU=\");\nvar _c;\n$RefreshReg$(_c, \"ThemeProvider\");\n\n\n;\n    // Wrapped in an IIFE to avoid polluting the global scope\n    ;\n    (function () {\n        var _a, _b;\n        // Legacy CSS implementations will `eval` browser code in a Node.js context\n        // to extract CSS. For backwards compatibility, we need to check we're in a\n        // browser context before continuing.\n        if (typeof self !== 'undefined' &&\n            // AMP / No-JS mode does not inject these helpers:\n            '$RefreshHelpers$' in self) {\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var currentExports = module.exports;\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var prevSignature = (_b = (_a = module.hot.data) === null || _a === void 0 ? void 0 : _a.prevSignature) !== null && _b !== void 0 ? _b : null;\n            // This cannot happen in MainTemplate because the exports mismatch between\n            // templating and execution.\n            self.$RefreshHelpers$.registerExportsForReactRefresh(currentExports, module.id);\n            // A module can be accepted automatically based on its exports, e.g. when\n            // it is a Refresh Boundary.\n            if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.isReactRefreshBoundary(currentExports)) {\n                // Save the previous exports signature on update so we can compare the boundary\n                // signatures. We avoid saving exports themselves since it causes memory leaks (https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/53797)\n                module.hot.dispose(function (data) {\n                    data.prevSignature =\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports);\n                });\n                // Unconditionally accept an update to this module, we'll check if it's\n                // still a Refresh Boundary later.\n                // @ts-ignore importMeta is replaced in the loader\n                module.hot.accept();\n                // This field is set when the previous version of this module was a\n                // Refresh Boundary, letting us know we need to check for invalidation or\n                // enqueue an update.\n                if (prevSignature !== null) {\n                    // A boundary can become ineligible if its exports are incompatible\n                    // with the previous exports.\n                    //\n                    // For example, if you add/remove/change exports, we'll want to\n                    // re-execute the importing modules, and force those components to\n                    // re-render. Similarly, if you convert a class component to a\n                    // function, we want to invalidate the boundary.\n                    if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.shouldInvalidateReactRefreshBoundary(prevSignature, self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports))) {\n                        module.hot.invalidate();\n                    }\n                    else {\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.scheduleUpdate();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            else {\n                // Since we just executed the code for the module, it's possible that the\n                // new exports made it ineligible for being a boundary.\n                // We only care about the case when we were _previously_ a boundary,\n                // because we already accepted this update (accidental side effect).\n                var isNoLongerABoundary = prevSignature !== null;\n                if (isNoLongerABoundary) {\n                    module.hot.invalidate();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    })();\n//# sourceURL=[module]\n//# sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;charset=utf-8;base64,\n//# sourceURL=webpack-internal:///(app-pages-browser)/./providers/ThemeProvider.tsx\n"));

(typeof document === \"undefined\") return;\n    const root = document.documentElement;\n    root.setAttribute(\"data-theme\", theme.themeMode);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-primary\", theme.primaryColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-accent\", theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-bg\", theme.bgColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-surface\", theme.surfaceColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-elevated\", theme.elevatedColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-primary\", theme.successColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-warning\", theme.warningColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-error\", theme.errorColor);\n For backwards compatibility, we need to check we're in a\n        // browser context before continuing.\n        if (typeof self !== 'undefined' &&\n            // AMP / No-JS mode does not inject these helpers:\n            '$RefreshHelpers$' in self) {\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var currentExports = module.exports;\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var prevSignature = (_b = (_a = module.hot.data) === null || _a === void 0 ? void 0 : _a.prevSignature) !== null && _b !== void 0 ? _b : null;\n            // This cannot happen in MainTemplate because the exports mismatch between\n            // templating and execution.\n            self.$RefreshHelpers$.registerExportsForReactRefresh(currentExports, module.id);\n            // A module can be accepted automatically based on its exports, e.g. when\n            // it is a Refresh Boundary.\n            if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.isReactRefreshBoundary(currentExports)) {\n                // Save the previous exports signature on update so we can compare the boundary\n                // signatures. We avoid saving exports themselves since it causes memory leaks (https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/53797)\n                module.hot.dispose(function (data) {\n                    data.prevSignature =\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports);\n                });\n                // Unconditionally accept an update to this module, we'll check if it's\n                // still a Refresh Boundary later.\n                // @ts-ignore importMeta is replaced in the loader\n                module.hot.accept();\n                // This field is set when the previous version of this module was a\n                // Refresh Boundary, letting us know we need to check for invalidation or\n                // enqueue an update.\n                if (prevSignature !== null) {\n                    // A boundary can become ineligible if its exports are incompatible\n                    // with the previous exports.\n                    //\n                    // For example, if you add/remove/change exports, we'll want to\n                    // re-execute the importing modules, and force those components to\n                    // re-render. Similarly, if you convert a class component to a\n                    // function, we want to invalidate the boundary.\n                    if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.shouldInvalidateReactRefreshBoundary(prevSignature, self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports))) {\n                        module.hot.invalidate();\n                    }\n                    else {\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.scheduleUpdate();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            else {\n                // Since we just executed the code for the module, it's possible that the\n                // new exports made it ineligible for being a boundary.\n                // We only care about the case when we were _previously_ a boundary,\n                // because we already accepted this update (accidental side effect).\n                var isNoLongerABoundary = prevSignature !== null;\n                if (isNoLongerABoundary) {\n                    module.hot.invalidate();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    })();\n//# sourceURL=[module]\n//# sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;charset=utf-8;base64,\n//# sourceURL=webpack-internal:///(app-pages-browser)/./providers/ThemeProvider.tsx\n"));

ThemeContext = /*#__PURE__*/ (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.createContext)(undefined);\n// Helper function to apply CSS variables directly to the DOM\nconst applyThemeToDOM = (theme)=>{\n    if (typeof document === \"undefined\") return;\n    const root = document.documentElement;\n    root.setAttribute(\"data-theme\", theme.themeMode);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-primary\", theme.primaryColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-accent\", theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-bg\", theme.bgColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-surface\", theme.surfaceColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-elevated\", theme.elevatedColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-primary\", theme.textPrimary);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-secondary\", theme.textSecondary);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-muted\", theme.textMuted);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-text-on-primary\", \"#ffffff\");\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-border\", theme.borderColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-success\", theme.successColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-warning\", theme.warningColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-error\", theme.errorColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-glass-bg\", theme.glassBg);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-primary-glow\", theme.primaryGlow);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-primary\", theme.chartPrimary || theme.primaryColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-secondary\", theme.chartSecondary || theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-chart-accent\", theme.accentColor);\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-radius\", \"\".concat(theme.radius, \"px\"));\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--prism-card-radius\", \"\".concat(theme.radius, \"px\"));\n    let fontVar = theme.font;\n    if (theme.font === \"Geist\") fontVar = \"var(--font-geist)\";\n    if (theme.font === \"JetBrains Mono\") fontVar = \"var(--font-geist-mono)\";\n    root.style.setProperty(\"--semantic-font\", fontVar);\n};\nconst useIsomorphicLayoutEffect =  true ? (react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1___default().useLayoutEffect) : 0;\nconst getInitialTheme = ()=>{\n    if (true) {\n        try {\n            const savedTheme = localStorage.getItem(\"prism-theme\");\n            if (savedTheme) {\n                return JSON.parse(savedTheme);\n            }\n        } catch (e) {\n            console.error(\"Failed to parse saved theme from localStorage\", e);\n        }\n    }\n    const firstPresetId = AVAILABLE_PRESETS[0].id;\n    return {\n        ...baseTheme,\n        ...presets[firstPresetId],\n        activePreset: firstPresetId\n    };\n};\nfunction ThemeProvider(param) {\n    let { children } = param;\n    _s();\n    const [themeState, setThemeState] = (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useState)(getInitialTheme);\n    const [mounted, setMounted] = (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useState)(false);\n    // useIsomorphicLayoutEffect runs synchronously before browser paint\n    useIsomorphicLayoutEffect(()=>{\n        let currentTheme = getInitialTheme();\n        setThemeState(currentTheme);\n        // Immediately apply CSS variables to the DOM before rendering frame\n        applyThemeToDOM(currentTheme);\n        setMounted(true);\n    }, []);\n    // Save to localStorage and apply CSS vars whenever themeState changes\n    (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useEffect)(()=>{\n        if (!mounted) return;\n        localStorage.setItem(\"prism-theme\", JSON.stringify(themeState));\n        applyThemeToDOM(themeState);\n    }, [\n        themeState,\n        mounted\n    ]);\n    const setTheme = (updates)=>{\n        setThemeState((prev)=>({\n                ...prev,\n                ...updates\n            }));\n    };\n    const applyPreset = (presetId)=>{\n        const presetTheme = presets[presetId];\n        if (presetTheme) {\n            setThemeState((prev)=>({\n                    ...prev,\n                    ...presetTheme,\n                    activePreset: presetId\n                }));\n        }\n    };\n    // We only render children once mounted to avoid hydration mismatch with text/content that depends on the theme.\n    // Wait, if we return null, there is a FOUC (white screen).\n    // The requirement says \"Avoid flashes of incorrect theme during page load\". \n    // Next.js hydration will just give warnings in dev, so we can suppress them or ignore them.\n    // Let's render children immediately. The CSS variables are applied synchronously above so there is no visual FOUC!\n    return /*#__PURE__*/ (0,react_jsx_dev_runtime__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_0__.jsxDEV)(ThemeContext.Provider, {\n        value: {\n            ...themeState,\n            setTheme,\n            applyPreset\n        },\n        children: /*#__PURE__*/ (0,react_jsx_dev_runtime__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_0__.jsxDEV)(\"div\", {\n            style: {\n                display: \"contents\"\n            },\n            suppressHydrationWarning: true,\n            children: children\n        }, void 0, false, {\n            fileName: \"C:\\\\Users\\\\Shaik Afreen\\\\OneDrive\\\\Desktop\\\\prism-v2-main\\\\providers\\\\ThemeProvider.tsx\",\n            lineNumber: 227,\n            columnNumber: 7\n        }, this)\n    }, void 0, false, {\n        fileName: \"C:\\\\Users\\\\Shaik Afreen\\\\OneDrive\\\\Desktop\\\\prism-v2-main\\\\providers\\\\ThemeProvider.tsx\",\n        lineNumber: 226,\n        columnNumber: 5\n    }, this);\n}\n_s(ThemeProvider, \"ImMjQcN2HTuMEUSRFxJ3tGNTIr0=\", false, function() {\n    return [\n        useIsomorphicLayoutEffect\n    ];\n});\n_c = ThemeProvider;\nfunction useTheme() {\n    _s1();\n    const context = (0,react__WEBPACK_IMPORTED_MODULE_1__.useContext)(ThemeContext);\n    if (context === undefined) {\n        throw new Error(\"useTheme must be used within a ThemeProvider\");\n    }\n    return context;\n}\n_s1(useTheme, \"b9L3QQ+jgeyIrH0NfHrJ8nn7VMU=\");\nvar _c;\n$RefreshReg$(_c, \"ThemeProvider\");\n\n\n;\n    // Wrapped in an IIFE to avoid polluting the global scope\n    ;\n    (function () {\n        var _a, _b;\n        // Legacy CSS implementations will `eval` browser code in a Node.js context\n        // to extract CSS. For backwards compatibility, we need to check we're in a\n        // browser context before continuing.\n        if (typeof self !== 'undefined' &&\n            // AMP / No-JS mode does not inject these helpers:\n            '$RefreshHelpers$' in self) {\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var currentExports = module.exports;\n            // @ts-ignore __webpack_module__ is global\n            var prevSignature = (_b = (_a = module.hot.data) === null || _a === void 0 ? void 0 : _a.prevSignature) !== null && _b !== void 0 ? _b : null;\n            // This cannot happen in MainTemplate because the exports mismatch between\n            // templating and execution.\n            self.$RefreshHelpers$.registerExportsForReactRefresh(currentExports, module.id);\n            // A module can be accepted automatically based on its exports, e.g. when\n            // it is a Refresh Boundary.\n            if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.isReactRefreshBoundary(currentExports)) {\n                // Save the previous exports signature on update so we can compare the boundary\n                // signatures. We avoid saving exports themselves since it causes memory leaks (https://github.com/vercel/next.js/pull/53797)\n                module.hot.dispose(function (data) {\n                    data.prevSignature =\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports);\n                });\n                // Unconditionally accept an update to this module, we'll check if it's\n                // still a Refresh Boundary later.\n                // @ts-ignore importMeta is replaced in the loader\n                module.hot.accept();\n                // This field is set when the previous version of this module was a\n                // Refresh Boundary, letting us know we need to check for invalidation or\n                // enqueue an update.\n                if (prevSignature !== null) {\n                    // A boundary can become ineligible if its exports are incompatible\n                    // with the previous exports.\n                    //\n                    // For example, if you add/remove/change exports, we'll want to\n                    // re-execute the importing modules, and force those components to\n                    // re-render. Similarly, if you convert a class component to a\n                    // function, we want to invalidate the boundary.\n                    if (self.$RefreshHelpers$.shouldInvalidateReactRefreshBoundary(prevSignature, self.$RefreshHelpers$.getRefreshBoundarySignature(currentExports))) {\n                        module.hot.invalidate();\n                    }\n                    else {\n                        self.$RefreshHelpers$.scheduleUpdate();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            else {\n                // Since we just executed the code for the module, it's possible that the\n                // new exports made it ineligible for being a boundary.\n                // We only care about the case when we were _previously_ a boundary,\n                // because we already accepted this update (accidental side effect).\n                var isNoLongerABoundary = prevSignature !== null;\n                if (isNoLongerABoundary) {\n                    module.hot.invalidate();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    })();\n//# sourceURL=[module]\n//# sourceMappingURL=data:application/json;charset=utf-8;base64,\n//# sourceURL=webpack-internal:///(app-pages-browser)/./providers/ThemeProvider.tsx\n"));
